title: Mastery Exam: CSS Units and Bounded Fluid Sizing
summary: Mastery Exam: CSS Units and Bounded Fluid Sizing retrieves prior skills in an original independent multi-medium sizing release decision scenario and requires implementation, behavior, failure-state, and transfer evidence.
objectives: Apply css absolute units, css font relative units, css percentage units, css viewport dynamic units, css math fluid sizing in unfamiliar constraints.
Produce or audit a real independent multi-medium sizing release decision artifact without happy-path-only reasoning.
Preserve earlier requirements while testing keyboard, narrow-width, invalid, and assistive-semantic states.

starterFiles.html: <!-- Continue the Community Needs Survey sizing work here. -->
starterFiles.css: /* Continue the bounded responsive sizing system here. */

steps.title: 01 · predict 1: screen and printed relief-pass sizing review · quiz
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 1: Which claim survives the changed case? Scenario: independent multi-medium sizing release decision.
steps.why: Mastery Exam: CSS Units and Bounded Fluid Sizing makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 1 in independent multi-medium sizing release decision.

content.title: independent multi-medium sizing release decision evidence
content.text: Choose the claim that preserves purpose, native behavior, and understandable relationships when presentation changes.

hints: Mastery Exam: CSS Units and Bounded Fluid Sizing checkpoint 1: identify the user task and data relationship first.
Mastery Exam: CSS Units and Bounded Fluid Sizing checkpoint 1: compare source, native behavior, and announced meaning.
Mastery Exam: CSS Units and Bounded Fluid Sizing checkpoint 1: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: Independent multi-medium sizing release decision case 1: cSS pixels always correspond to one physical device pixel.
Independent multi-medium sizing release decision case 1: choose px for suitable screen-relative details and physical units for controlled print constraints, then verify zoom and actual output.
Independent multi-medium sizing release decision case 1: use centimeters for all screen text because physical measurements are always more consistent.

options.text: independent multi-medium sizing release decision phase 2.1: identify the property and the element where it is declared
independent multi-medium sizing release decision phase 2.2: name the root element or font metric that supplies its reference
independent multi-medium sizing release decision phase 2.3: calculate nested and inherited results
independent multi-medium sizing release decision phase 2.4: change root font size content and language and inspect the outcome

steps.title: 03 · inspect 3: nested survey panel percentage overflow incident · quiz
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 3: Which claim survives the changed case? Scenario: independent multi-medium sizing release decision.

content.text: Choose the claim that preserves purpose, native behavior, and understandable relationships when presentation changes.

hints: Mastery Exam: CSS Units and Bounded Fluid Sizing checkpoint 3: identify the user task and data relationship first.

Mastery Exam: CSS Units and Bounded Fluid Sizing checkpoint 3: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: Independent multi-medium sizing release decision case 3: every percentage resolves against viewport width.
Independent multi-medium sizing release decision case 3: identify the property, containing block, writing mode, and definite dimensions, then calculate and measure the percentage result.
Independent multi-medium sizing release decision case 3: assume fifty percent means half the element’s own current size for every property.

steps.title: 04 · answer 4: mobile browser controls obscuring survey actions · quiz
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 4: Which claim survives the changed case? Scenario: independent multi-medium sizing release decision.
steps.why: Mastery Exam: CSS Units and Bounded Fluid Sizing makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 4 in independent multi-medium sizing release decision.

content.title: independent multi-medium sizing release decision evidence
content.text: Choose the claim that preserves purpose, native behavior, and understandable relationships when presentation changes.

hints: Mastery Exam: CSS Units and Bounded Fluid Sizing checkpoint 4: identify the user task and data relationship first.
Mastery Exam: CSS Units and Bounded Fluid Sizing checkpoint 4: compare source, native behavior, and announced meaning.
Mastery Exam: CSS Units and Bounded Fluid Sizing checkpoint 4: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: Independent multi-medium sizing release decision case 4: one hundred vh always equals the visible mobile screen height.
Independent multi-medium sizing release decision case 4: use natural content flow first, choose small or dynamic viewport units for a proven need, and verify controls never obscure or trap content.
Independent multi-medium sizing release decision case 4: use 100vw for every page wrapper even when a scrollbar consumes inline space.

steps.title: 05 · answer 5: survey heading and spacing fluidity review · quiz
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 5: Which claim survives the changed case? Scenario: independent multi-medium sizing release decision.

content.text: Choose the claim that preserves purpose, native behavior, and understandable relationships when presentation changes.

hints: Mastery Exam: CSS Units and Bounded Fluid Sizing checkpoint 5: identify the user task and data relationship first.
Mastery Exam: CSS Units and Bounded Fluid Sizing checkpoint 5: compare source, native behavior, and announced meaning.
Mastery Exam: CSS Units and Bounded Fluid Sizing checkpoint 5: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: Independent multi-medium sizing release decision case 5: fluid values need no minimum or maximum because every viewport scales proportionally.
Independent multi-medium sizing release decision case 5: write the minimum and maximum from usability constraints, build the preferred expression, and test every bound plus zoom and content changes.
Independent multi-medium sizing release decision case 5: use only viewport-width text so every line scales in exact proportion to the screen.

options.text: Independent multi-medium sizing release decision case 6: cSS pixels always correspond to one physical device pixel.

options.text: Independent multi-medium sizing release decision case 8: every percentage resolves against viewport width.

options.text: Independent multi-medium sizing release decision case 9: one hundred vh always equals the visible mobile screen height.
